prs.title: [ROCm] Use supports_fp8() for FP8 feature gates instead of arch checks
prs.author: laudney
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Replace verbose architecture-specific FP8 capability checks (`on_gfx9()`, `on_mi3xx()`, `has_device_capability(94)`) with the cross-platform `current_platform.supports_fp8()` predicate across all FP8-related code paths.

This is a small refactoring PR that **unblocks FP8 features on RDNA4 (gfx12) GPUs** which support FP8 but were excluded by MI300-specific gates. The `supports_fp8()` method already correctly covers MI300, gfx950, gfx12 on ROCm and capability >= 8.9 on CUDA — this PR simply switches the callers to use it.

### Changes (5 files, net -27 lines)

| File | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| `fused_batched_moe.py` | 10-line `on_gfx9()` + CUDA capability check | `supports_fp8()` one-liner |
| `fused_moe.py` | Same 10-line pattern | `supports_fp8()` one-liner |
| `scaled_mm/rocm.py` | `on_mi3xx()` — excluded gfx12 | `supports_fp8()` — includes gfx12 |
| `scaled_mm/pytorch.py` | `on_mi3xx()` + `capability >= 94` | `supports_fp8()` |
| `ptpc_fp8.py` | `has_device_capability(94)` | `supports_fp8()` + updated docstring |

### Why this matters

Without this change, RDNA4 GPUs fall through to non-FP8 paths even though they have hardware FP8 support (`v_dot4_f32_fp8_fp8`, FP8 format in `torch._scaled_mm`). The old `on_mi3xx()`/`on_gfx9()` gates were written before RDNA4 existed.

### Related PRs (RDNA4/gfx12 series)
- #34709 — [ROCm] Enable wvSplitK/wvSplitKQ skinny GEMM kernels for RDNA4
- #34632 — [ROCm] Add MXFP4 inline dequant Triton kernel for RDNA4/gfx12
- #34631 — [ROCm] Make Whisper causal attention backend-agnostic

## Test plan
- [x] Qwen3-14B-FP8 (block-wise FP8) serving on gfx1201 — FP8 rowwise matmul path now activates
- [x] Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B AWQ-4bit — FP8 MoE gate correctly enables Triton FP8 experts
- [ ] No regression on MI300-series (predicate returns same result as old checks)
- [ ] No regression on CUDA (predicate delegates to `has_device_capability(8, 9)`)

prs.title: [Bug] Fix GLM-5.1 running error on ROCm platform
prs.author: qli88
prs.body_head:
1. AITER MLA implementation requires num_heads>=16. Add padding when necessary for both regular MLA and sparse MLA;
2. An AITER bug will cause divided_by_zero error when num_heads is too small. Set it to num_heads to circumvent, and will remove it when corresponding AITER PR gets merged.

## Test Plan
__To verify sparse mla padding works correctly:__
1. **Padding added since MLA num_heads==8:**
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 vllm serve /data/GLM-5.1-FP8/ -tp 8 --trust-remote-code  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8

2. **No padding since MLA num_heads==16:**
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 vllm serve /data/GLM-5.1-FP8/ -tp 4 --trust-remote-code  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8

__To verify regular mla padding works correctly:__

3. **No padding since MLA num_heads==16:**

VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 vllm serve /data/DeepSeek-R1/ -tp 8 --trust-remote-code  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8

__Accuracy test command:__
lm_eval --model local-completions \
  --tasks gsm8k \
  --output_path ./results \
  --log_samples \
  --num_fewshot 20 \
  --model_args model=/data/GLM-5.1-FP8/,base_url=http://localhost:8000/v1/completions,num_concurrent=5,max_retries=3,timeout=3000,seed=1234

## Test Result

1. GLM 5.1, TP=8

|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|    20|exact_match|↑  |0.9447|±  |0.0063|
|     |       |strict-match    |    20|exact_match|↑  |0.9447|±  |0.0063|

2. GLM 5.1, TP=4

|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-----:|
|gsm8k|      3|flexible-extract|    20|exact_match|↑  |0.8795|±  |0.0090|
|     |       |strict-match    |    20|exact_match|↑  |0.8764|±  |0.0091|

3. DS R1, TP=8

|Tasks|Version|     Filter     |n-shot|  Metric   |   |Value |   |Stderr|
|-----|------:|----------------|-----:|-----------|---|-----:|---|-

prs.title: [ROCm] Rename LLMM1 to vecMatMul, refactor, and fix RDNA4 correctness bugs
prs.author: wjabbour
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Renames `LLMM1`/`LLGemm1_kernel` → `vecMatMul`/`vecMatMul_kernel` across the codebase, extracts helper functions (`load_tile`, `dot_row`, `warp_reduce`), replaces magic numbers with named constants, and adds stage-level comments documenting the four-stage reduction.

Also fixes two silent correctness bugs on RDNA4 (WARP_SIZE=32) that produced wrong results while passing on CDNA CI hardware:

- **Row write bug**: `lane % 32 == 0` hardcoded as the half2 write guard; on WARP_SIZE=32 rows beyond the first pair were never written. Fixed to `lane % (2 * THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP)`.
- **Cross-warp reduction bug**: reduction loop bounded by `THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP` instead of `num_warps`, silently dropping warp contributions when K > 2048. Fixed with a strided loop.

Regression tests for both bugs are included, verified red on unfixed code and green on the fix. Tests always pass on CDNA (WARP_SIZE=64) CI hardware — the comments explain why.

Also adds explicit `TORCH_CHECK` guards for `K % 8 == 0`, `M % rows_per_block == 0`, `K <= 8192`, and `rows_per_block ∈ {2, 4, 8, 16}`, and replaces the silent wrong `default` case in the dispatch switch with a hard error.

## Test plan
- `pytest tests/kernels/quantization/test_rocm_skinny_gemms.py -v` on gfx1201 (RDNA4, WARP_SIZE=32)
- 76/76 vecMatMul tests passing; regression tests confirmed red/green on unfixed/fixed code
- Not duplicating any open PR (supersedes #40770, which was opened from main by mistake)
- AI assistance used (Claude); all changed lines reviewed by submitter

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

prs.title: [ROCm] Rename LLMM1 to vecMatMul, refactor, and fix RDNA4 correctness bugs
prs.author: wjabbour
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Renames `LLMM1`/`LLGemm1_kernel` → `vecMatMul`/`vecMatMul_kernel` across the codebase, extracts helper functions (`load_tile`, `dot_row`, `warp_reduce`), replaces magic numbers with named constants, and adds stage-level comments documenting the four-stage reduction.

Also fixes two silent correctness bugs on RDNA4 (WARP_SIZE=32) that produced wrong results while passing on CDNA CI hardware:

- **Row write bug**: `lane % 32 == 0` hardcoded as the half2 write guard; on WARP_SIZE=32 rows beyond the first pair were never written. Fixed to `lane % (2 * THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP)`.
- **Cross-warp reduction bug**: reduction loop bounded by `THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP` instead of `num_warps`, silently dropping warp contributions when K > 2048. Fixed with a strided loop.

Regression tests for both bugs are included, verified red on unfixed code and green on the fix. Tests always pass on CDNA (WARP_SIZE=64) CI hardware — the comments explain why.

## Test plan
- `pytest tests/kernels/quantization/test_rocm_skinny_gemms.py -v` on gfx1201 (RDNA4, WARP_SIZE=32)
- 76/76 vecMatMul tests passing; regression tests confirmed red/green on unfixed/fixed code
- Not duplicating any open PR (checked via `gh pr list --search "LLMM1 OR LLGemm1 OR vecMatMul"`)
- AI assistance used (Claude); all changed lines reviewed by submitter

prs.title: [LoRA] MoE LoRA Refactor
prs.author: jeejeelee
prs.body_head:
## Motivation


Currently, MoE LoRA is wired in by monkey-patching methods on the modular kernel at construction time. `FusedMoEWithLoRA._inject_lora_into_fused_moe` wraps `FusedMoEKernel.apply`, `TritonExperts.activation`, and `TritonExperts.moe_sum` with `fwd_decorator` / `act_decorator` / `moe_sum_decorator`, and smuggles tensors between them through  `moe_state_dict`. This has several concrete problems:

### 1. Hacky and hard to maintain.
The LoRA contribution is hidden inside decorators on base-MoE methods. Someone reading the MoE experts code sees `self.activation(...)` and `self.moe_sum(...)` — they have no syntactic hint that these calls may actually run LoRA shrink/expand GEMMs, or that  `moe_state_dict` carries cross-call state between them. Debugging requires holding the patching order in your head.

### 2. MoE changes don't see LoRA
 Because the LoRA path lives outside the expert `apply()` functions, any refactor of `TritonExperts` / `UnfusedOAITritonExperts` / `MarlinExperts` has to re-discover the LoRA contract (what `activation` receives, what `moe_sum` receives, what shape `intermediate_cache*` has). Every MoE-side change risks breaking LoRA  silently.

### 3. Hard to extend
  Adding support for new features — EP, additional quantized backends, new expert impls — means replicating or working around the decorator chain, and the state-dict plumbing assumes one specific control flow. There is no extension point for an expert that wants to apply LoRA at a different point .

## Change


Treat LoRA as a `first-class` concern in the modular MoE kernel rather than an external patch.

### 1. `MoELoRAContext`: a single explicit payload

A new `MoELoRAContext` dataclass (`vllm/lora/lora_context.py`) packages all of
the LoRA state a MoE forward pass needs:

- `w13_lora_a_stacked` / `w13_lora_b_stacked` / `w2_lora_a_stacked` /
  `w2_lora_b_stacked`
- `adapter_enabled`, `max_loras`
- routing/sharding info: `top_k`, `w13_num_slices`

prs.title: [Core][WIP] Check for GPU<->CPU sync during CI
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).

prs.title: [ROCm] Expanded sparse MLA support
prs.author: ekuznetsov139
prs.body_head:
This PR:
* Fixes a bug in cache_kernels.cu that resulted in incorrect results for block_stride not multiple of 16
* Adds block size 16 to supported sparse MLA block sizes on ROCm
* Fixes a few bugs in the Python code
* Adds optimized Triton kernels with support of arbitrary block size for two of the steps
* In case of rocm_fp8_paged_mqa_logits, routes to the Triton kernel by default, since AITER  is slower for block size 1 and it does not handle block size 16 correctly

I've verified that, after this PR, both GLM-5-FP8 and DeepSeek-V3.2 work correctly on ROCm (MI325  / gfx942) with both --block-size 1 and --block-size 16.

prs.title: [Build] Add Python 3.14 to supported version list.
prs.author: nascheme
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

This allows vLLM to built from source using Python 3.14.  This requires that the numba PR (#34764) is merged first.

Note that Python 3.13 would still be the recommended interpreter and that "ray" is not available yet for 3.14.

## Test Plan

Run builtin test suite with pytest.  E.g. `pytest src/vllm/tests`.

## Test Result

Most unit tests pass.  I have the following test failures:
```
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_ca_buffer_sharing.py - ValueError: Error initializing torch.distributed using env:// rendezvous: environment variable RANK expected, but not set
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_multiproc_executor.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_torchrun_example.py - ValueError: Error initializing torch.distributed using env:// rendezvous: environment variable RANK expected, but not set
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/distributed/test_torchrun_example_moe.py - ValueError: Error initializing torch.distributed using env:// rendezvous: environment variable RANK expected, but not set
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/entrypoints/openai/test_openai_schema.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/entrypoints/test_grpc_server.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/kernels/helion/test_utils.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/plugins_tests/test_stats_logger_plugins.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/utils_/test_mem_utils.py
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/v1/engine/test_engine_core_client.py - OSError: You are trying to access a gated repo.
ERROR vllm/vllm/tests/v1/kv_connector/nixl_integration/test_edge_cases.py - ValueError: Please set the PREFILL_PORT, DECODE_PORT, and PROXY_PORT.
```
Looking at the CI scripting inside `./buildkite`, I gather these tests need either different hardware or some different pre-setup to pass.
